query_normalized: the impact of visual attention on recall of emotional stimuli in rapid visual sequences psychology
rationale: The paper addresses "visual attention" as a computational mechanism in deep learning (specifically self-attention in neural networks for image processing), which is a distinct construct from the user's question regarding human "visual attention" as a psychological cognitive process affecting memory recall. This falls under the rejection rule for distinct constructs sharing only homonym keywords (e.g., "attention" in AI vs. "attention" in psychology).
